title: June Inflection Point Remains Significant
summary:
Main
By Jeffrey Landsberg
Overall dry bulk rates increased as we anticipated last week, with only panamax rates declining. China's commodity demand has remained strong, a surge of Indonesian spot coal cargoes has emerged and has been only the latest surge in a specific spot cargo volume, canal issues have continued to help reduce spot supply, and the industrial sector has continued to improve globally, The capesize panamax ratio has also been far too narrow in previous weeks, and this has also been a factor for capesize rates increasing and panamax rates decreasing as we have discussed in Commodore Research's most recent Weekly Dry Bulk Report. As we also have been discussing in our Weekly Dry Bulk Reports, we remain bullish for the dry bulk market.
